input.prompt: I need to convert this entire repository to Central Package Management. The solution is at advanced-multi-complexity/Enterprise.slnx. There are some complications: package versions defined as MSBuild properties, conditional package references for multi-targeting, and a shared Common.props that adds a package to all projects. Inline MSBuild property values and use the highest version for any conflicts.

data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Let me analyze and implement the CPM conversion.

**Package inventory & conflict resolution (highest wins):**
| Package | Versions found | CPM Version |
|---|---|---|
| System.Text.Json | 8.0.5, 9.0.0, 10.0.1 | **10.0.1** |
| Azure.Storage.Blobs | 12.19.0, $(BlobsVersion)=12.24.0 | **12.24.0** |
| Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson | 6.0.36, 8.0.11 | **8.0.11** |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | $(HostingVersion)=8.0.1 | **8.0.1** |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Logging | 9.0.0 | **9.0.0** |
| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | 1.15.0 | **1.15.0** |
| xunit | 2.9.3 | **2.9.3** |

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Storage.Blobs" Version="12.24.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="8.0.11" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me analyze all the packages and their versions:

**From Common.props** (imported by all projects via Directory.Build.props):
- Microsoft.Extensions.Logging: 9.0.0

**From Directory.Build.props**:
- Properties: BlobsVersion=12.24.0, HostingVersion=8.0.1

**Core.csproj**:
- System.Text.Json: 9.0.0
- Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting: $(HostingVersion) → 8.0.1

**Api.csproj** (multi-targeting net8.0;net6.0):


- System.Text.Json: 10.0.1
- Azure.Storage.Blobs: $(BlobsVersion) → 12.24.0
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson: 6.0.36 for net6.0 and 8.0.11 for net8.0

**Web.csproj**:
- System.Text.Json: 10.0.1
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.0

**Legacy.csproj**: System.Text.Json: 8.0.5 and Azure.Storage.Blobs: 12.19.0

**Tests.csproj**: xunit 2.9.3 and System.Text.Json: 10.0.1

For the CPM migration, I need to create a Directory.Packages.props file with all packages using the highest version when there are conflicts, remove version attributes from individual project files, enable central package management, replace any MSBuild property references with their actual values, and clean up the version properties from Directory.Build.props. Looking at the conflicts, System.Text.Json has versions 8.0.5 and 10.0.1, so I'll use 10.0.1 as the highest.

Now I'm resolving the remaining package versions: Azure.Storage.Blobs goes to 12.24.0, Microsoft.Extensions.Logging stays at 9.0.0, and Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting is 8.0.1. But there's a complication with Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson—it has different versions (6.0.36 and 8.0.11) that are conditional on the target framework, and CPM only allows one version per package in the central manifest. The user wants me to use the highest version when there are conflicts, so I'll go with 8.0.11 even though it might not be compatible with net6.0. Now I'm setting up the Directory.Packages.props file with the centralized version declarations for all the packages.

I also need to update Directory.Build.props to remove the old version properties and keep just the implicit usings setting, then clean up Common.props to remove version attributes from the PackageReference entries since those will now be managed centrally. Finishing the Api.csproj configuration with package references for both target frameworks. I'm noticing that both conditional ItemGroups reference the same package, so with Central Package Management providing a single version, both net6.0 and net8.0 will end up using the same version anyway. Defining the project files...

    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

result.detailedContent: Converting to Central Package Management

toolArgs.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Storage.Blobs" Version="12.24.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="8.0.11" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />

    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
